translation.literal_translation: The eater (is Brahman), because of taking the moving and unmoving. (9)
translation.technical_translation: Sutra 1.2.9: attā is identified as Brahman on the basis of charachara-grahana (all-inclusive object scope).

lexical_notes.gloss: because of inclusion of moving and unmoving
lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Sutra-hetu establishing atta as Brahman through total object-scope.

lexical_notes.gloss: concise inferential reason in sutra form
lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Captures the compressed logic where one linga settles referent identity.
Semantic breadth that excludes finite candidates for the eater.

lexical_notes.gloss: determination of the eater as Brahman
lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Result secured by the charachara indicator in this sutra.

uncertainty_notes: Curated in contiguous S02 pass with stable cadence and inferential-tag schema.
A later philological pass should revisit upadhi/vyavahara terminology and atta-grahana technical choices in records 1.2.016-1.2.022.
